body:
This adds in the spherical shell domain (thank you ClimaAtmos) and adds support for it in our soil RHS functions. We also add in tests using the spherical shell.

The RHS functions which specify a boundary flux as a WVector (rather than a Covariant3Vector - as our flux is a scalar * gradient of a scalar) seem to be fine. Internally, this must be converted to a Covariant3Vector. the test `test/Soil/soil_bc/jl` shows that you obtain the same outcome regardless of how you pass in the boundary flux vector, provided that you adjust for the difference in scale.

**We have to revisit this once we allow for topography.**
title: Add cubed sphere domain
